title: packera thurberi
intro: Packera thurberi, also known as Western Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids. The health benefits of P. thurberi include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties. P. thurberi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been used in various herbal preparations, including creams and salves, to support skin health and wound healing.
